reply:
, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to help his mom in the kitchen. One day, his mom was making a cake. Tim wanted to help, so his mom let him measure the flour.
Tim used the measure to measure the flour. He put the measure in the big bowl. He was very careful, but the mix did not go very far. Tim was happy to help his mom. He knew he was doing a good job.
When the cake was done, Tim and his mom put it in the oven. The cake smelled so good! Tim's mom was very proud of him. They waited for the cake to cool down. When the cake was done, they took it out of the oven. It was a big, yummy cake. Tim and his mom shared the cake with their family, and everyone was very happy.

prompt: A little girl find a bird on the tree
reply:
. She looks up and down, and makes a funny noise. The bird sees the noise and flies down. It pecks at the tree.
"Hey, bird, what are you doing?" Anna says. She is afraid of the bird. She drops the stick and runs to the tree.
"I'm sorry, bird. I was playing with the stick. I did not mean to scare you. Please don't be mad at me," Anna says.
The bird looks at Anna and sees her kind eyes. It feels sorry for Anna and wants to be her friend. It flies down from the tree and gives her a hug. It rubs its head against her cheek and nuzzles her cheek.
"Thank you, bird. You are very kind. I like you too," Anna says.
She smiles and says, "You're welcome, bird. You are very sweet. Can we be friends?"
The bird nods and flies away. Anna and the bird play with the stick and the bird. They have fun and make noises. They are happy.

prompt: Once upon a time, a king
reply:
lived in a big castle. The king was very tall and had a shiny crown. He loved to play with his friends in the garden.
One day, the king was very tired. He decided to take a nap under a big tree. While he was sleeping, a little mouse came to the castle. The mouse saw the king and thought he was a big cat. The mouse was scared and wanted to run away.
The king woke up and saw the mouse. He was not scared of the mouse. The mouse and the king became friends. They played together in the garden every day. The mouse was not scared anymore. They were very happy.
